published_prior_art.cite: ChernozhukovEtAl2018DML: cross-fit orthogonal-score ATE/product-rate inference, with LRO/ADML papers providing adjacent common-EIF pieces

repo_prior_art.cite: eid_bjs_efficient_event_study: analogous IF-frontier proposal for staggered DiD, not DML/LRO/ADML ATE

published_prior_art.cite: Velez2024DML: DML1/DML2 growing-K separation; vanDerLaanLuedtkeCarone2024CDML: calibrated one-slow-nuisance inference; neither gives a DML-LRO-ADML spectral iff frontier

repo_prior_art.cite: eid_lp_svar_nonequiv and eid_bjs_efficient_event_study: analogous requests for primitive non-equivalence witnesses, not this ATE/Riesz witness

published_prior_art.cite: BrunsSmithDukesFellerOgburn2024ABW: algebraic equivalences for augmented balancing/AutoDML-style estimators, not a finite-support DML-LRO-ADML strict-risk witness

soundness_flags.label: Corollary: No residual Riesz direction
soundness_flags.one_line: The reduction uses equality of projection spans to conclude no positive L-A gap eigenvalue, but equal spans do not imply Pi_L,n and Pi_A,n agree as operators; require operator equality or zero range of Pi_L,n - Pi_A,n on the gap subspace.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Patch the no-gap corollary by replacing span equality with an explicit null pairwise projection-gap condition, then restate the LRO-ADML collapse as range(Pi_L,n - Pi_A,n)=0 on selected coordinates.
Add one displayed comparison table separating the proposed threshold from Velez2024DML growing-K, C-DML one-slow-nuisance calibration, and regularized Riesz effective-dimension results.

For the witness conjecture, specify a nondegenerate three-point template with positive residual variance in both treatment arms and active D-L and L-A singular directions, so Stage 0 has a concrete target rather than only inequalities.

recommended_next_step: Revise the LRO-ADML no-gap sanity reduction; the novelty kernel otherwise clears the repo and published axes at the requested flagship floor.
